headline: June jobs data unveils a harsh truth for Bitcoin bulls
summary: Bitcoin (BTC) traders got a mixed signal on July 1 after ADP reported that private-sector employment grew by just 98,000 in June. It missed the 110,000 Dow Jones consensus and was down from an unrevised 122,000 in May. The ADP National Employment Report offers an independent, frequently ...

headline: Bitcoin Pops Off 21-Month Low to $60K as Soft Data Eases Rate-Hike Fears
summary: BTC recovered from local lows to $60,000 after softer U.S. jobs and factory data revived hopes the Fed's hawkish turn could be easing.

headline: What investors should take away from Warsh's Sintra speech
summary: Market Catalyst Host Brooke DiPalma is joined by Manulife John Hancock Investments Co-Chief Investment Strategist Emily Roland to break down Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's remarks from Sintra, Portugal, and what they signal for inflation, interest rates, and the outlook for future Fed rate cuts.

headline: Stocks Dip to Start Second Half
summary: Stocks dipped at the open on Wednesday to kick off the second half of the year. The Dow fell 214 points, or 0.4%. The S&P 500 was down 0.6%. The Nasdaq was down 0.8%. Markets opened as Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh appeared on a panel with other global central bank leaders.

headline: ADP National Employment Report: Private Sector Employment Increased by 98,000 Jobs in June; Annual Pay was Up 4.4%
summary: Private sector employment increased by 98,000 jobs in June and pay was up 4.4 percent year-over-year according to the June ADP National Employment Report® produced by ADP Research in collaboration with the Stanford Digital Economy Lab ("Stanford Lab").

headline: ADP (ADP) Stock Trades At a Discount On a 26% Slump
summary: Automatic Data Processing stock has had a tough 12 months with the share price down 25.8% over the year, even as its valuation checks signal a more mixed picture rather than a clear warning sign or a clear bargain. Over the past year, Automatic Data Processing has declined 25.8%, which puts recent returns sharply at odds with its longer term 3 and 5 year gains. Recent employment data from the ADP National Employment Report and a rating upgrade from Wells Fargo can support sentiment around...
